metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing: (1) the two-phase run_det_floor contract (static P1–P9 + dynamic project-DET phase, fail-open per-check), (2) the exec:DET LLM-batch exclusion rule (filter sites in orchestrator.route_criteria and workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria), and (3) the plan-time DET scoping semantics (file_impact-scoped / advisory-repo-context / N/A-fail-open).
findings.finding: The plan introduces several novel architectural decisions — a two-phase run_det_floor (static P1–P9 + dynamic project-check phase), a new exec:DET descriptor branch, a new routing schema shape (detector selector + fail_mode), DET-tier LLM-batch isolation semantics, and plan-time scoping rules (file_impact-scoped / advisory / N/A) — but specifies no update to any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc to capture these decisions for future maintainers.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## DET-tier isolation, thin-slice sequencing & schema details (remediation, run 4)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/adr/NNN-det-project-invariants.md` (or a CLAUDE.md section under a `## DET criteria` heading) covering: the two-phase det_floor contract; the invariant that exec:DET descriptors are consumed only by the det-floor project-check phase and never routed to the LLM batch; the three-way plan-time scoping rule; and the fail_mode defaulting contract (absent → open for project criteria, explicit closed for security class).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the new `_descriptor_from_prompt` DET branch inserts its `exec == 'DET'` guard BEFORE the `prompts.get_prompt(...)` call (line 344) — not after — so an activated project DET criterion never attempts to load a non-existent prompt file.
findings.finding: `_descriptor_from_prompt` unconditionally calls `prompts.get_prompt(...)` (line 344) before any exec-tier check, so a project DET criterion activated in `effective_criteria` will cause `load_criteria` to raise a `RegistryError` (prompt file missing) before the new DET branch can intercept it. The plan says to add 'a `_descriptor_from_prompt` DET branch that skips `get_prompt`', but the branch must guard the `get_prompt` call itself, not just change the return value.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_descriptor_from_prompt`, read `routing_map.get(cid)` first (already done for the `None` check), then branch: `if routing and routing.get('exec', '').upper() == 'DET': return { 'id': cid, 'exec': 'DET', 'name': ..., 'scenario': ..., ... }` — before the `prompts.get_prompt(...)` call.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `_resolve_criteria()` in `ProductionBatchRunner` also skips descriptors with `exec == 'DET'` (co-locate the DET-exclusion rule with every `exec_tier()` call-site), or make `exec_tier()` return a distinct `'DET'` string so callers can filter by tier-equality rather than relying on each site to know the fallthrough behavior.
findings.finding: The DET-tier isolation rule ('exec:DET descriptors never enter the LLM batch') is specified for `route_criteria` and `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria`, but is NOT specified for `ProductionBatchRunner._resolve_criteria()` (production_batch_runner.py:153-203), which is a separate, independent routing path that also calls `registry.exec_tier()`. Confirmed in code: `exec_tier()` at registry.py:519-525 falls through to return `'1-TURN'` for `exec=='DET'` (not AGENT, not 2-STEP → default). A `project.` DET criterion activated via `_project_criteria()` would pass through `_resolve_criteria()` and be classified as `1-TURN`, entering the LLM `single` list.
findings.location: Plan section 'DET-tier isolation, thin-slice sequencing & schema details (remediation, run 4)' → 'exec:DET criteria never enter the LLM batch'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) update `exec_tier()` to return `'DET'` for `exec=='DET'` and add `if registry.exec_tier(desc) == 'DET': skipped.append(cid); continue` in `_resolve_criteria()`; or (b) add a guard at the top of `_resolve_criteria()` mirroring the one added to `route_criteria`. The plan should name `_resolve_criteria` as a required filter site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `DetResult` field values (status, blocking, finding) for each of the three plan-review scoping outcomes: file_impact-scoped match (status='fail', blocking=per-posture, finding=...), advisory repo-context (status='fail', blocking=False, finding=advisory-text), and N/A-fail-open (status='abstain', blocking=False, finding=None).
findings.finding: The `DetResult` field values for the 'advisory repo-context' output (when `file_impact` is absent) are never specified. The plan says this case is 'advisory-only over the repo context, never a hard block,' but `det_floor.py:712-728` partitions results by exact field values: `det_blocking_findings` uses `r.blocked` (= `r.status=='fail' AND r.blocking==True`); `det_advisory_findings` uses `r.status=='fail' AND NOT r.blocking`. A `status='pass'` yields no finding at all; a `status='fail', blocking=False` with a non-None `finding` yields an advisory. The plan does not commit which of these the consumer emits for the advisory and N/A-fail-open cases.
findings.location: Plan section 'Match-scoping without a diff (clears G6 blocker 1)' — advisory/N-A cases; AC 'Plan-review DET-invariant matches are scoped to the ticket's file_impact'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table or code sketch in the integration-point section mapping each scoping outcome to its DetResult fields, referencing the existing `det_advisory_findings`/`det_blocking_findings` partition logic so the new consumer's output is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `DetResult` field values (status, blocking, finding, coverage) for the 'no file_impact → advisory' case so implementers and the downstream partition agree on its classification.
findings.finding: The 'advisory' DetResult returned when no file_impact is present is described narratively ('advisory-only', 'never a hard block') but the exact field values (status, blocking, finding, coverage) for that case are not specified anywhere in the plan. An implementer must infer the field values, risking inconsistency with how the downstream partition (det_advisory_findings) classifies it.
findings.location: Plan-review consumer integration — 'If there is no file_impact, the DET invariant is advisory-only over the repo context'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'In the advisory (no file_impact) case, return DetResult(id=<criterion_id>, name=<rule_message>, status="advisory", blocking=False, finding=None, coverage="advisory") — this routes to det_advisory_findings and contributes no coverage signal.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies exec:DET criteria are absent from the LLM batch fan-out (route_criteria / include_* set), matching the test described in the run-4 body.
findings.finding: The 'exec:DET criteria never enter the LLM batch' deliverable — described in detail in the 'DET-tier isolation' section of run 4 as 'clears G6', specifying changes to `orchestrator.route_criteria` and `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria` — has no covering acceptance criterion. It is a non-trivial, architecturally load-bearing requirement (a DET criterion accidentally reaching `pass1_chunk` would be a correctness bug), yet none of the AC blocks reference it.
findings.location: Remediation run 4 — 'exec:DET criteria never enter the LLM batch' sub-section; Acceptance Criteria blocks (all rounds)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the run-4 AC block: '- [ ] An activated `exec: DET` project criterion appears in `run_det_floor` results and is ABSENT from `route_criteria` / `include_*` LLM fan-out; a test asserts this isolation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that verifies the security-class `id_prefix` selector in `criteria_routing.json` produces byte-identical scan results to the former `SECURITY_DETECTOR_PREFIX` constant, covering both removal of the constant and correctness of the replacement.
findings.finding: The migration of the security-class scan consumer from the hardcoded `SECURITY_DETECTOR_PREFIX` constant to a data-driven `id_prefix: "rebar.builtin.security."` selector in `criteria_routing.json` is not verified by any AC for both the removal AND the replacement producing identical match behavior. AC1 only tests that a new project invariant routes correctly; no AC asserts the security-class id_prefix selector matches exactly the same detector set as the former constant.
findings.location: AC block — original AC1 ('no hardcoded rebar.builtin.security. prefix'); 'Data-driven map location' section; 'Schema + migration details' (id_prefix shape)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 or add: '- [ ] The security-class `id_prefix: "rebar.builtin.security."` routing entry matches the same detector set as the removed `SECURITY_DETECTOR_PREFIX` constant; the existing `test_code_review_ws5.py` fail-closed tests pass unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one new test (separate from the existing `test_code_review_ws5.py` suite) that loads a fixture `criteria_routing.json` containing a custom DET routing entry and asserts that `run_detectors` resolves the criterion and fail_mode from the JSON map — proving the data-driven path end-to-end without the hardcoded constant.
findings.finding: The MVP step relies on existing `test_code_review_ws5.py` tests to prove the hardcoded-prefix→data-driven-routing cutover, but those tests were designed for the OLD hardcoded path; no new test is mentioned that specifically loads a custom detector selector from `criteria_routing.json` and asserts routing flows through the JSON-driven lookup — the existing suite could go green with the hardcoded constant still in place or with the JSON path mocked away, leaving the data-driven routing unverified.
findings.location: Schema + migration details / Thin-slice sequencing step 1 (MVP) / AC: 'no hardcoded rebar.builtin.security. prefix'
findings.suggested_fix: In the MVP test, construct a minimal in-memory or fixture `criteria_routing.json` with an entry like `{"id": "project.test_invariant", "exec": "DET", "detector": {"id_prefix": "test.prefix."}, "fail_mode": "open"}`, wire it into the consumer, and assert that a scan hit on a `test.prefix.*` detector surfaces a DetResult for `project.test_invariant` — fully bypassing the hardcoded constant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm each LLM-routing callsite (`route_criteria`, `plan_review_assemble_criteria`, `production_batch_runner._project_criteria`) uses the descriptor's raw `c.get('exec','').upper() == 'DET'` check — not `registry.exec_tier(c)` — to exclude DET criteria, since `exec_tier` currently maps DET→1-TURN silently.
findings.finding: `exec_tier()` (line 519-525) does not return `'DET'`; it silently maps `exec=='DET'` to `'1-TURN'` (the final `else` branch). The plan's DET-isolation filter in `route_criteria` and `plan_review_assemble_criteria` depends on checking `exec == 'DET'` on the descriptor, but the descriptor's `exec` field comes from the routing entry — as long as the routing entry carries `exec: 'DET'`, the filter can compare `c.get('exec', '').upper() == 'DET'` directly without using `exec_tier()`. However, the plan must NOT route through `exec_tier()` for DET criteria or they silently become single-turn rubric calls.
findings.location: ## DET-tier isolation — exec:DET criteria never enter the LLM batch; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:519-525 (exec_tier)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the complete `detector` sub-object schema co-located with `_validate_routing_entry`: specify which keys are legal (`id`, `id_prefix`), that exactly one must be present (mutual exclusion), what error is raised for a missing/malformed sub-object, and whether the sub-object is required on every `exec:DET` routing entry.
findings.finding: The `detector` sub-object schema added to DET routing entries (described as `detector: {id?: str, id_prefix?: str}`) is never fully defined: the plan does not specify the exact JSON key name in the routing entry, what happens when both `id` and `id_prefix` are present simultaneously, whether the sub-object is required or optional for a DET entry, or what `_validate_routing_entry` should raise for a malformed/missing sub-object. The plan states `_validate_routing_entry gains fail_mode ∈ {open, closed} validation` but is silent on `detector` sub-object validation rules.
findings.location: Plan section 'DET-tier isolation, thin-slice sequencing & schema details (remediation, run 4)' → 'Schema + migration details' → 'detector selector shape'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Schema + migration details' section: '`detector` is REQUIRED on every DET routing entry; exactly one of `id` or `id_prefix` must be set (both present → `_validate_routing_entry` raises RegistryError; neither present → RegistryError). `_validate_routing_entry` checks this immediately after `fail_mode`.' Also update `_validate_routing_entry` to enforce this.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge AC2 and AC5 (or differentiate them explicitly): if AC5 is meant to cover infrastructure-failure triggers specifically, rewrite it as 'Fail-open is preserved specifically when the engine binary is absent, a parse error occurs, or a timeout fires — distinct from a normal abstain — for fail_mode:open criteria.'
findings.finding: Original AC2 ('Per-criterion fail_mode honored: project invariants default fail-open; the security class stays fail-closed') and original AC5 ('Fail-open is preserved on engine-binary-absent / parse-error / timeout for fail_mode:open') are near-duplicate requirements covering the same behavior — both assert that a `fail_mode:open` criterion abstains to skip=PASS. AC5 adds only the trigger-condition enumeration (absent-binary, parse-error, timeout), which is already implied by AC2's 'abstain→skip=PASS' language.
findings.location: Original Acceptance Criteria — AC2 and AC5

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
